icpPillars.title: Three types of clients. One service.

pillars.title: Funded founders in Tokyo
pillars.desc: You just closed a pre-seed or seed round. You need a bilingual product online before your runway narrows. Japanese agencies quote ¥8M and 4 months.

pillars.title: Companies entering Japan
pillars.desc: Your HQ approved the Japan launch budget. You need a bilingual site and someone who understands JP legal requirements — before the launch date.

pillars.title: Japanese startups going global
pillars.desc: Your product is JP-only. Investors keep asking for an English version. You need it before the next fundraising round.

deliverablePillars.title: Everything a bilingual product needs. Nothing you don't.

pillars.title: EN/JA from day one
pillars.desc: Not translated after the fact. Bilingual routing, locale-aware components, and JP-style UX built into the architecture from the start.

pillars.title: Production-grade stack
pillars.desc: Next.js App Router + Supabase + Stripe. The same stack that runs ZeroEn and WebMori — auth, billing, and i18n production-ready.

pillars.title: Japan compliance built in
pillars.desc: Tokushoho (特定商取引法), JP invoice format, JP-style billing flows. Required for any Japanese e-commerce or SaaS.

meta.title: Pricing — ZeroEn
meta.description: Fixed-price bilingual web products for funded founders and serious businesses in Tokyo. Starter ¥380k, Growth ¥880k, MVP Build from ¥1.5M.

pricing.title:
Fixed price.
No equity. No surprises.
pricing.subtitle: Compare ¥880k to a Japanese agency quote (typically ¥5–10M for equivalent scope), or hiring a bilingual full-stack engineer for 3 months (¥2–3M all-in).
pricing.retainerNote: Retainers cover hosting, monitoring, monthly updates, and priority support. Cancel with 30 days notice.

outOfScope.title: Out-of-scope work
outOfScope.note: All out-of-scope requests are quoted before work starts. You approve before we bill.

faqs.q: What's included in the fixed price?
faqs.a: Everything in the tier's feature list. Scope is locked at kickoff. Any work beyond the locked scope is quoted at ¥15,000/hr before work starts — you approve before we bill.

faqs.q: Do you take equity or revenue share?
faqs.a: No. Fixed price only. We don't take equity, SAFE notes, or revenue share. You own the business; we deliver the product.
You do, after final payment. Code ownership transfers on delivery — no lock-in, no subscription required to keep your site running.

faqs.q: What does 'bilingual from day one' mean?
faqs.a: Every component is built locale-aware using next-intl. EN and JA routing, fonts (Murecho for JP), date/number formatting, and JP-specific UX patterns are architecture decisions — not afterthoughts.
Book a 30-minute scoping call. We discuss your project, confirm ICP fit, and send a fixed-price proposal within 48 hours.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Five steps from first call to live product.

steps.name: Scoping call
steps.desc: 30-minute call to discuss your project, confirm ICP fit, and understand the scope. No commitment required.

steps.name: Fixed-price proposal
steps.desc: Within 48 hours of the call, you receive a fixed-price proposal with locked scope, timeline, and milestone payment schedule.

steps.name: Kickoff
steps.desc: Sign the proposal, pay the first milestone. We confirm all assets, content, and requirements before the first line of code.

steps.name: Build & delivery
steps.desc: Development proceeds against the locked scope. You review a staging deployment before final delivery. Anything beyond scope is quoted first.

steps.name: Launch
steps.desc: Final payment on delivery. Code ownership transfers to you. Optional monthly retainer for hosting, updates, and support.

terms.title:
The deal,
in plain English.
terms.subtitle: Standard terms for every ZeroEn client. No surprises.

items.label: Platform fee
items.value: ¥10,000+/mo after your MVP launches. Covers hosting, changes, and analytics.

items.label: Scope freeze
items.value: MVP scope locked at kickoff. New features = per-request charge.

items.label: Kill switch
items.value: 90 days unpaid → agreement terminates. We keep the code.

items.label: IP
items.value: ZeroEn retains code ownership. You license the live site via active subscription.

items.label: Liability
items.value: ZeroEn's liability is capped at fees paid in the prior 12 months.

items.label: Portfolio rights
items.value: We always retain the right to showcase this work.

sections.title: 1. Future Equity (Phase 2)
sections.body: Equity arrangements (SAFE notes, revenue share) are not part of Phase 1 agreements. ZeroEn currently operates as a subscription hosting service. Equity terms may be offered on select Phase 2 deals and will be documented in a separate, individually signed agreement at that time. Nothing in these terms grants or implies any equity interest.

sections.title: 2. Revenue Share (Phase 2)
sections.body: Revenue share arrangements are not active in Phase 1. If a revenue share is agreed for a specific client, it will be documented in a separate written agreement signed by both parties before it takes effect. These standard terms do not constitute a revenue share agreement.

sections.title: 3. Platform Fee
sections.body: After the MVP launches, a monthly subscription fee of ¥10,000+ applies (Basic or Growth plan). This covers: hosting on ZeroEn's Vercel Pro account, included monthly changes per plan, and a monthly analytics report. The subscription begins 30 days after launch. Payment is via Stripe. The client's application remains live only while the subscription is active. ZeroEn's total liability under this agreement is limited to the fees paid by the client in the 12 months preceding any claim. ZeroEn provides the service "as is" and makes no warranties beyond uptime of the underlying Vercel infrastructure.

sections.title: 4. Scope Freeze
sections.body: The MVP scope is agreed and locked at the kickoff call. This scope is documented and signed by both parties. Any features, changes, or additions beyond the agreed scope are treated as per-request charges. ZeroEn is not obligated to build out-of-scope work. Per-request charges are quoted upfront and must be paid before work begins.

sections.title: 5. Per-Request Charges
sections.body: Work beyond the locked MVP scope is available as per-request charges: Small changes (~30 min) ¥4,000; Medium changes (1–3 hours) ¥10,000 (Growth plan only); Large builds (half day+) ¥25,000+. All quotes are provided upfront. Work begins only after payment is received.

sections.title: 6. Kill Switch
sections.body: If the platform fee remains unpaid for 90 consecutive days, the agreement terminates automatically. Upon termination: the client's hosted application is taken offline, ZeroEn retains full rights to the codebase. ZeroEn will make reasonable efforts to notify the client before reaching the 90-day threshold.

sections.title: 7. Reversion Clause
sections.body: If the client does not launch their application within 6 months of the agreed build completion date, full code rights revert to ZeroEn. The client may request an extension in writing. Extensions are granted at ZeroEn's discretion.

sections.title: 8. IP Ownership
sections.body: ZeroEn retains full ownership of all code, designs, and assets created under this agreement. The client receives a non-exclusive license to use the live hosted application for as long as the platform fee is paid. This license is non-transferable and does not include access to or transfer of source code. ZeroEn always retains the right to use the work in its portfolio and marketing materials, including screenshots, case studies, and build logs.

sections.title: 9. Service Standards
sections.body: ZeroEn will make reasonable efforts to keep the client's application live and performant on Vercel's infrastructure. If the client is unresponsive for 6 or more consecutive months (no replies to communications, no active use of the service), ZeroEn may pause the service with 14 days written notice. This clause exists to ensure ZeroEn can manage its active client roster effectively.

sections.title: 10. Governing Law
sections.body: These terms are governed by the laws of Japan. Any disputes are resolved first through good-faith negotiation, then through the Tokyo District Court if necessary. ZeroEn and the client agree to resolve disputes without litigation where possible.

sections.title: 11. Confidentiality & Non-Disclosure
sections.body:
Both ZeroEn and the client agree to keep each other's confidential information private. Confidential information includes: application data, business plans, technical specifications, financial projections, user research, and any other information shared during the application, onboarding, or build process.

Neither party will share, copy, or use the other's confidential information without written consent. Standard exclusions apply: information that is publicly available, independently developed, or legally required to be disclosed is not covered by this clause.

Data deletion: any applicant — accepted or rejected — may request deletion of all their submitted data at any time. Rejected application data is automatically deleted within 30 days of the rejection decision. Requests can be sent to the operator directly.

This confidentiality obligation survives the termination of any agreement between ZeroEn and the client. Governing law for this clause follows Section 10.

sections.title: 1. Who We Are
sections.body: ZeroEn ("we", "us", "our") is a web development and hosting service operated by a Canadian sole proprietorship. Our website is zeroen.dev. For privacy inquiries, contact: hello@zeroen.dev.

sections.title: 2. Data We Collect
sections.body:
We collect the following information when you use our service:

**Account data:** Name, email address, and password (managed via Supabase Auth). You may also sign in with Google or GitHub, in which case we receive your public profile information from that provider.

**Project data:** Website preferences, content, and feedback you provide during onboarding and change requests.

**Payment data:** Billing is processed by Stripe. We store your Stripe Customer ID to link payments to your account. We do not store full card numbers or CVV codes — these are handled entirely by Stripe.

**Usage data:** Server logs, page views, and analytics collected by Vercel Analytics (anonymised, no cross-site tracking).

**Communications:** Messages you send through the dashboard are stored to maintain your project history.

sections.title: 3. How We Use Your Data
sections.body:
We use your data to:
- Provide, maintain, and improve the ZeroEn service
- Process subscription payments and issue invoices
- Communicate about your project status, change requests, and billing
- Send transactional emails (project updates, payment confirmations) via Resend
- Comply with legal obligations

sections.title: 4. Third-Party Services
sections.body:
We share data with the following processors:

**Supabase** (database and authentication) — supabase.com/privacy
**Stripe** (payment processing) — stripe.com/privacy
**Vercel** (hosting and analytics) — vercel.com/legal/privacy-policy
**Resend** (transactional email) — resend.com/legal/privacy-policy
**GitHub** (optional OAuth login) — docs.github.com/en/site-policy/privacy-policies/github-privacy-statement

We do not sell your data to any third party.

sections.title: 5. Data Retention
sections.body: We retain your account and project data for the duration of your subscription plus 90 days after termination, to allow for reactivation. Payment records are retained for 7 years as required by tax law. You may request deletion of your data at any time by emailing hello@zeroen.dev.

sections.title: 6. Your Rights
sections.body:
Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to:
- Access the personal data we hold about you
- Correct inaccurate data
- Request deletion of your data
- Object to or restrict processing
- Receive your data in a portable format

To exercise any of these rights, email hello@zeroen.dev.

sections.title: 7. Cookies
sections.body: We use essential session cookies for authentication. We do not use advertising or tracking cookies. Vercel Analytics uses anonymised, cookie-free metrics.

sections.title: 8. Security
sections.body: Data is encrypted in transit (TLS) and at rest. Access to production systems is restricted to the operator. We follow security best practices including regular dependency audits.

sections.title: 9. Changes to This Policy
sections.body: We may update this policy from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via the dashboard. The current version is always available at zeroen.dev/privacy.

sections.title: 10. Contact
sections.body: For any privacy-related questions or requests: hello@zeroen.dev

sections.title: Pricing
sections.body:
**Starter:** ¥380,000 (tax included) — one-time build fee
**Growth:** ¥880,000 (tax included) — one-time build fee
**MVP / SaaS Build:** ¥1,500,000 and up (tax included) — one-time build fee

**Optional monthly retainer:**
Starter: ¥15,000/month · Growth: ¥35,000/month · MVP: ¥80,000–¥150,000/month

**Out-of-scope work:** ¥15,000/hour (quoted before work begins)

⚠️ TODO-REVIEW: Confirm pricing figures and tax treatment with operator before publishing.

sections.title: Additional Fees
sections.body: None. Internet connection and data charges are the customer's responsibility.

sections.title: Payment Method
sections.body: Credit card (Stripe)

sections.title: Payment Timing
sections.body: Milestone-based. First charge is collected at kickoff per the milestone schedule in the signed proposal. Retainer charges (if applicable) are made monthly from the retainer start date.

sections.title: Service Delivery
sections.body: Service is provided following contract conclusion and first milestone payment. Starter tier: approximately 14 business days. Growth tier: 21–28 business days. MVP / SaaS Build: 6–8 weeks. Exact timeline confirmed in the signed proposal.

sections.title: Cancellation Policy
sections.body:
**Milestone refund policy:**
Kickoff milestone (first payment): 50% refund if cancelled before work begins, non-refundable after work begins.
Subsequent milestones: non-refundable once the relevant milestone deliverable has been approved.
Final milestone: non-refundable.

**Retainer cancellation:** 30 days written notice required. No minimum term.

**Code ownership:** Transfers to client upon final milestone payment.

⚠️ TODO-REVIEW: Confirm refund and cancellation terms with operator before publishing.

sections.body: Latest version of a modern browser (Chrome / Safari / Edge / Firefox)
